name: E001 sales triple stack: region + product + month + threshold
prompt: North region Widget units above 100 in the first quarter

name: E002 traffic category + threshold + quarter
prompt: Organic visits over 1000 in Q1

name: E003 traffic category + threshold + weekday filter
prompt: Paid channel signups under 20 on Mondays

name: E004 readings sensor + null-prone measure + threshold + date window
prompt: Sensor A temperature above 15 between March 1 and March 7

name: E005 traffic negation channel + month + threshold
prompt: non-Organic visits over 800 in August

name: E006 traffic category + fuzzy-number threshold + month
prompt: Social visits over 1.2k in July
note: Social visits never exceed 1.2k -> renders empty or constraint-dropped

name: E007 sales region exclusion + product + month range
prompt: every region but North, Gadget units, from March to June

name: E008 traffic category + threshold-with-comma + quarter
prompt: Email visits of at least 1,200.50 in Q4
note: Email never reaches 1,200.50 -> impossible threshold (comma+decimal parse tested deterministically)

name: E009 traffic exclude-weekends + channel + threshold
prompt: Paid visits over 700 excluding weekends in the last week of March
note: Paid>700 on late-March weekdays -> impossible/near-empty

name: E010 readings sensor + humidity threshold + temperature threshold + day
prompt: Sensor B humidity over 60 where temperature is at least 14 on the 10th
note: triple filter on a single day -> near-empty

name: E011 sales run-on region + product + month + threshold messy
prompt: show me revenue for South region Gizmo only in Q2 but just the months where units are over 50

name: E012 traffic empty-stack zero rows
prompt: Email visits over 5000 in January on Sundays
note: Email>5000 -> impossible

name: E013 traffic channel + threshold + single-day range
prompt: Organic signups over 30 on 2024-08-15

name: E014 sales pie after stacked filter
prompt: pie of revenue by product for West region with units above 80 in the second half of the year

prompt: the channel with the lowest average visits — break its visits down by weekday
